sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports acne located on the face, which started about two years ago and is present most every day. He has been using Persa-gel and washing regularly, which is somewhat helpful. He reports no associated symptoms including itching, bleeding, or pain. The patient feels greasy skin in the morning. He uses Elf facial cleanser in the morning and an elf facial cleanser called the super clarity cleanser at night. He uses Persa-gel in the evening. The patient reports that his acne flares with his periods, but not really. He has been getting a regular period for about two years.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: A full exam was performed today, except for under the underwear and under the bra. Multiple benign nevi were observed on the trunk and extremities. Scattered skin-colored papules, open and closed comedones, and erythrema papules were noted on the face, primarily on the forehead and central cheeks and chin. The chest and back are relatively spared. The remainder of the examination is normal. The patient's skin is dry.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has mild to moderate acne, primarily comedonal with a small inflammatory component. The patient has multiple benign moles on their arms, legs, back, and abdomen.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I would like to start with a topical therapy first. Every morning, you will wash your face with a mild cleanser then use a moisturizer labeled, "noncomedogenic," with sunscreen SPF 30 or higher. This means it won't clog your pores. Now, in the evening, wash your face with the same cleanser and allow it to dry. Apply adapalene, 0.1% cream, in a thin layer to the areas you generally get acne. I want you to start off using this a few nights a week and slowly work up to using it every night. If it is ... excuse me, if it is very expensive or not covered by insurance, you can try different gel over the counter. You can follow that with clean and clear Persa-gel in a thin layer, or where you generally get acne. And then a noncomedogenic moisturizer. You're atopic retinoid will cause some sensitivity, so you will need to wear sunscreen when you are outside. It may also cause some dryness or irritation. Order sunscreen SPF 30 or higher. Follow up in six months to assess progress. Schedule a follow-up appointment in six months.
